title: Nursing Assistant Practitioner
how_to_become: You'll need a healthcare-related qualification at SCQF level 8 or equivalent, or relevant previous experience demonstrated through recognition of prior learning.
description: Nursing assistant practitioners carry out routine care and treatment with patients in hospital and community settings. Supervised by registered nurses, they provide person-centred, safe, and effective care responsive to individual needs, preferences, and values, including clinical observations, reporting on a patient's condition, and escalating concerns.
role_overview: As a nursing assistant practitioner, you'll have direct contact with patients, families, and carers, working in a multidisciplinary team. You'll carry out delegated clinical tasks, evaluate patient responses to treatment or rehabilitation, report progress to a registered nurse, supervise healthcare support workers, and assist with nursing students' education and training.

entry_requirements.overview: Healthcare-related qualifications at SCQF level 8 or equivalent, or relevant experience demonstrated through recognition of prior learning.
prior_experience_examples: Knowledge and understanding of procedures and delivering treatment plans
Working in an integrated multidisciplinary team
Supervision of healthcare support workers and students

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland. Some nursing assistant practitioners start as healthcare support workers.

learning_and_development.overview: Nursing assistant practitioners must keep knowledge and skills up to date through reflective practice and CPD.

career_progression.overview: Nursing assistant practitioners can take on more responsibilities, move to associate practice educator roles, or become a registered nurse.
progression_routes: Registered nurse (via pre-registration undergraduate nursing degree — 3 years full-time, with possible entry to year 2 based on accreditation of qualifications and experience)

chunks.text: To become a nursing assistant practitioner, you'll need a healthcare-related qualification at SCQF level 8 or equivalent, or relevant experience demonstrated through recognition of prior learning.

Nursing assistant practitioners carry out routine care and treatment in hospital and community settings, supervised by registered nurses, providing person-centred, safe, and effective care including clinical observations and escalating concerns.

Nursing assistant practitioners carry out delegated clinical tasks, evaluate patient responses to treatment, report to registered nurses, supervise healthcare support workers, and assist with nursing students' education and training.

A healthcare-related qualification at SCQF level 8 or equivalent is required, or relevant experience including working in multidisciplinary teams, managing a delegated caseload, supervising support workers, and reflective practice.
Mandatory training includes health and safety, infection control, and moving and handling. Role-specific training may include higher clinical skills and medicines administration.

Nursing assistant practitioners can progress to associate practice educator roles or become a registered nurse via a pre-registration undergraduate nursing degree. Accreditation of qualifications and experience may provide entry to year 2.
